data.name: CF-Access-CredStuffing — Login Brute Force / Credential Stuffing on portal.novamind.ai
data.description: Detects credential stuffing and brute-force login abuse against the NovaMind employee portal (class_uid 4002, 'HTTP Requests'). Isolates authentication endpoints (url_string contains '/login') and failed/throttled responses (unmapped.EdgeResponseStatus in 401/403/429, evaluated as a string), then groups by src_ip + host and fires when a single source produces >= 10 failed login responses in the 5-minute window. Also surfaces distinct_uas as a corroborating signal: many failures with a rotating User-Agent from one IP is classic stuffing tooling. Low false-positive because a legitimate user fat-fingering a password does not produce 10+ 401/403/429s in five minutes from one IP; the internal >=10 floor is the effective threshold (rule threshold kept at >0 so the query's own filter governs). MITRE ATT&CK T1110.004 (Credential Stuffing) and T1110.001 (Password Guessing). Covers OneFlare scenario 04.

scheduledParams.query:
class_uid=4002 dataSource.cloudflare_dataset='HTTP Requests'
| let src_ip = src_endpoint.ip
| let host   = http_request.url.hostname
| let uri    = http_request.url.url_string
| let status = unmapped.EdgeResponseStatus
| filter uri contains:anycase("/login")
| filter status in ('401','403','429')
| group
    failed_logins = count(),
    distinct_uas  = estimate_distinct(http_request.user_agent),
    sample_status = any(status),
    sample_ua     = any(http_request.user_agent),
    country       = any(src_endpoint.location.country),
    first_seen    = oldest(timestamp),
    last_seen     = newest(timestamp)
  by src_ip, host
| filter failed_logins >= 10
| let detection_time = simpledateformat(last_seen, 'yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss z')
| sort -failed_logins
| columns detection_time, src_ip, host, failed_logins, distinct_uas, sample_status, sample_ua, country, first_seen
| limit 100
